plant.description:
Brief Description and Common Forms
Description: The avocado is a fleshy fruit known for its unusually high fat content2.... The anatomy of the plant includes the fruit, a large central seed (or pit), leaves, stems, and bark5.... As a natural defense mechanism, the avocado plant produces a fungicidal toxin called persin, which is highly toxic to many animals23.
Common Forms: Avocados are most frequently consumed by humans as fresh, raw fruit flesh, mashed into dishes such as guacamole, or extracted into pure avocado oil6....

toxins.name: Persin
toxins.description:
in cats: Cats are particularly sensitive to persin because their livers are less efficient at breaking the compound down3. When ingested, the toxin acts as a severe gastrointestinal irritant, causing abdominal pain, vomiting, and diarrhea13. If ingested in large quantities, persin has the potential to cause systemic fluid accumulation, respiratory distress, and myocardial damage (necrosis of the heart muscle)2.... In lactating animals, persin can also cause sterile mastitis, which involves the necrosis and hemorrhage of the mammary gland epithelium24.
Any notes on concentration or potency: The highest and most toxic concentrations of persin are located in the leaves, bark, stems, skin (peel), and pits (seeds) of the avocado plant1.... The edible green flesh contains only trace amounts of persin38. While cats are at risk of persin poisoning, their overall susceptibility to lethal cardiac complications is considered lower than that of highly vulnerable species like birds, rodents, and livestock
toxins.concentration_notes: or potency: The highest and most toxic concentrations of persin are located in the leaves, bark, stems, skin (peel), and pits (seeds) of the avocado plant1.... The edible green flesh contains only trace amounts of persin38. While cats are at risk of persin poisoning, their overall susceptibility to lethal cardiac complications is considered lower than that of highly vulnerable species like birds, rodents, and livestock

toxins.name: High Fat Content (Dietary Lipids) (Note
toxins.description:
in cats: Feline digestive systems are delicate and not adapted to digest large amounts of plant-based fats16. Ingesting avocado flesh or pure avocado oil can severely irritate the gastrointestinal tract and trigger an inflammatory cascade in the pancreas, leading to a painful and potentially life-threatening condition called acute pancreatitis1.... Furthermore, if a cat develops gastrointestinal upset from the fat and stops eating (anorexia), their body will rapidly mobilize peripheral fat stores to the liver for energy10. Because the feline liver is inefficient at processing these fats without adequate dietary protein, the fats accumulate in the liver cells, causing a secondary, life-threatening crisis known as hepatic lipidosis (fatty liver disease)210.
Any notes on concentration or potency: Avocado flesh and extracted avocado oils are exceptionally dense in fat and calories911. Due to this concentration, even the flesh—which is largely free of the persin toxin—poses a high risk of pancreatitis and severe gastrointestinal distress, particularly for senior cats or those with a history of digestive issues212. Veterinarians recommend limiting any accidental exposure to a tiny fraction (no more than 10–15 grams) to prevent these metabolic consequences
toxins.concentration_notes: or potency: Avocado flesh and extracted avocado oils are exceptionally dense in fat and calories911. Due to this concentration, even the flesh—which is largely free of the persin toxin—poses a high risk of pancreatitis and severe gastrointestinal distress, particularly for senior cats or those with a history of digestive issues212. Veterinarians recommend limiting any accidental exposure to a tiny fraction (no more than 10–15 grams) to prevent these metabolic consequences

symptoms.name: Vomiting
symptoms.notes: Cats may vomit due to the high fat content or persin

symptoms.name: Pancreatitis
symptoms.notes: Caused by the very high fat content in avocado flesh
